operation: First, a 10 x 10 rectangular sketch is created on the initial base workplane. A second workplane is placed 10 units offset above the base, with a centered 5 x 5 rectangular sketch added to it. A third workplane is placed another 10 units offset above the second workplane (20 units total above the base), with a second centered 5 x 5 rectangular sketch added. Finally, a loft operation is executed across all three sketched profiles to generate the continuous solid 3D part.
geometry: This is a vertically oriented lofted solid with square cross sections. The largest square cross section sits at the bottom of the part, tapering smoothly inward to a smaller square cross section at the halfway point of the part's total height. The upper half of the part retains the smaller uniform square cross section from the midpoint to the top face, with all cross sections aligned concentrically along the same central vertical axis.
function: This tapered post component is intended for use as a support standoff, furniture foot, or alignment pin, with a wider base for improved mounting stability and a narrower upper section to fit smaller mating parts or reduce overall material usage.
